what_it_does: This file is a YAML-frontmatter ledger record that registers an image attachment in the design-source channel, pointing to the image asset at image://design-source/deck1-2026/p-12 and recording when it was added.
observations: The file contains only YAML frontmatter with no body content
It declares an attachment id att-f80df858
The target is an image:// URI with a deck/page path deck1-2026/p-12
standouts: References a paginated design deck image at deck1-2026/p-12
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata
Fields include id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added
Target uses an image:// URI scheme
concerns: Record is minimal and lacks a caption or description
Target URI cannot be verified from this file alone
No author or provenance context is recorded
No checksum or media format information
notes: A bare attachment ledger entry that links the design-source channel to a specific page image in a 2026 deck.
questions: What content is shown in deck1-2026/p-12?
Who added this attachment and for what decision?
Is images a controlled attachment_type value?
Are there related attachments for other pages of deck1-2026?
purpose_vs_actual: No explicit purpose is stated, but the content matches the apparent role of an attachment ledger entry; it records a typed link to an image asset.
intention_signals: It appears intended to catalog and timestamp a specific image asset from a design deck so it can be located and referenced within the channel memory ledger.
novelty: Uses a paginated deck image URI (deck1-2026/p-12) as the attachment target, suggesting a structured deck/page asset model.
contribution: Provides a timestamped, typed pointer to a design-source image asset, supporting retrieval and provenance tracking for deck page 12 without asserting priority over other records.
summary_for_embedding: Attachment ledger record att-f80df858 in the design-source channel points to image://design-source/deck1-2026/p-12 and records an images attachment added on 2026-06-22T11:31:00Z.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to catalog a specific image asset from a 2026 design deck into the channel memory ledger.
